ideation: This sequence mimics a cricket rubbing its legs together to make a chirping sound.  Since our robot doesn't have legs, we'll simulate this by having the robot's arms mimic the motion. The animation will focus on the left arm, which will move in a circular motion, while the right arm remains relatively still for contrast. The sequence will use small, precise movements to suggest the delicate nature of the cricket's action. Frame 1: Both arms are at rest. Frame 2: The left arm begins to move upward and inward, simulating the first part of the rubbing motion. Frame 3: The left arm continues its circular motion, moving downward and outward. Frame 4: The left arm returns to a position near its starting point, completing the cycle. Frame 5: Both arms return to rest.

frames.beat: arms rest, a cricket poised to chirp
left arm begins its delicate rubbing motion upward
the motion continues downward, a soft rasping sound
the cycle completes, a brief pause
both arms settle back into stillness
